reviews.review_description: Not quite sure what was happening, but this is very colorful and nice.

I just cannot get this to install on my Go. If I click on install whilst in the Go, nothing happens. If I try to install via the Oculus app it keeps saying that my headset is not connected - even though it is. I am not having any issues installing other apps.
Doesn’t work got to pick up a cup then could not do anything else all you could do is look around with the flash light in your hand but couldn’t move or pick up anything including the beer cup

It’s a pleasure to see artists use certain platforms to complement their art style and to expand their audience. Such is the case with the creators of Raddlesham Mumps on the Oculus Go. This is not a long experience (around 15 or so minutes) and it is not a game, but rather an interactive story. We see in the promotional materials that this experience is but a mere component in an overall multimedia universe for this piece of fiction, and in VR it is quite intriguing. We get Tim Burton-esque visuals and storytelling, while the poet has a haunting and rhythmic quality a la Graeme Edge of The Moody Blues. I enjoyed it, however brief it was, and just may investigate further into the Raddlesham Mumps mythology. It can be enjoyed by just about anyone of any age group who enjoy fantasy and gothic storytelling. It’s shallow and the story here feels fragmented, robbing it of cohesion and complexity, but it remains nonetheless amusing and compelling. For that, I recommend it at the free price tag.
